bullet_points: Implement a data loader for patch-based loading of signal and noise maps.
Compute empirical noise covariance matrices for SO LAT bands (90, 150, 217 GHz) in harmonic (Fourier) space.
Construct cILC weights in harmonic space using spectral response vectors from utils.tsz and utils.ksz.
Verify the CMB-nulling constraint analytically and save the weights and covariance matrices.
Convert CIB auxiliary channels (353–857 GHz) from Jy/sr to µK_CMB using utils.jysr2uk.
Account for beam smoothing differences between Planck-band (4.42–4.72 arcmin) and SO-band channels when constructing spectral difference maps.
Normalize spectral difference maps and CIB channels using local variance estimates.
Apply robust scaling to CIB maps and save the processed feature tensors.
Split the 1523 patches into explicit train (70%), validation (15%), and test (15%) sets.
Compute normalization statistics (mean, variance) exclusively from the training set.
Standardize tSZ ground truth maps and generate binary significance masks (y > 10^-7).
Implement data augmentation (rotations/flips) for the training set and save processed targets/masks.
Define the multi-scale U-Net architecture with gated cross-attention.
Implement the composite loss function (pixel-wise L1-reconstruction and spectral consistency via utils.powers).
Configure the training loop with Focal Loss, gradient clipping, and AdamW optimizer.
Save the model architecture and loss definitions for the training step.
Execute the training process on the GPU using the defined architecture and loss.
Monitor performance on both significant and null patches.
Save the model checkpoint with the best validation loss for subsequent fine-tuning.
Implement a differentiable Pseudo-Cl estimator operating on 2D FFTs of 256x256 patches, binning multipoles into annular rings.
Integrate a wavelet-based loss targeting angular scales between 1 and 5 arcmin (3–4 dyadic scales).
Perform a final fine-tuning pass on the best-checkpoint model using these physical constraints.
sub_tasks.sub_task: Physical Consistency and Power Spectrum Constraints

bullet_points: Train a control model without CIB features using the same train/validation/test split and random seeds as Step 5.
Inject GNFW cluster profiles (log M_500 from 13.5 to 15.0) into noise-dominated inputs to determine the reconstruction threshold.
Compute performance metrics (r_ell) for both models.

Generate a single multi-panel figure: (a) ground-truth vs. reconstructed tSZ maps, (b) r_ell for full vs. ablated model, (c) residual bias vs. tSZ intensity, and (d) reconstructed vs. ground-truth power spectra.
Save all diagnostic plots and summary statistics.
Synthesize quantitative results (r_ell at specific bins, bias percentages, minimum detectable cluster mass, performance gain from CIB).
Interpret the impact of CIB features and discuss physical validity in the context of FLAMINGO.
Write the full Results section with subsections: cILC baseline, SR-DAE reconstruction, ablation, and cluster detection threshold.
